about.content: I am studying a double degree in Computer Engineering and Mathematics, as well as a Master in Statistical Techniques. I am a person passionate about technology, eager for action and constantly learning. Throughout my training, I have studied Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Statistics both self-taught and through various internships in companies.

education.degree: Double bachelor's degree in Informatics Engineering and Mathematics

Developed a simple model to forecast discounted sales during the first two weeks of sales periods.

experience.description: Involved in a project targeting anomaly detection and missing values imputation within the field of process mining. Designed and evaluated hybrid deep learning models with variational autoencoders and normalizing flows for event log reconstruction.

Worked with Snowflake for efficient data management.
Created interactive dashboards using PowerBI and Streamlit.

experience.description: Strengthened my foundations in ML by an in-depth whole read of "Introduction to Statistical Learning" (Hastie's book) and subsequently employed this knowledge to construct a model for predicting housing prices in my city.
responsibilities: Studied statistical learning concepts for ML applications.
Developed a housing price prediction model.
Utilized PowerBI and Streamlit for data visualization.

experience.title: CiTIUS Summer Fellowships

responsibilities: Developed an AI model for heart disease classification.
Implemented RNNs with an attention mechanism.
Engaged in research paper analysis for model improvement.

projects.title: Efficient semantic segmentation of land cover images using an encoder-decoder architecture
projects.description: My Informatics Engineering Bachelor's Thesis focused on semantic segmentation of multispectral land cover images using encoder-decoder neural network architectures. I explored various deep encoder-decoder architectures to maximize segmentation quality. Additionally, I optimized computational efficiency by improving training and inference times, particularly in a multi-GPU setting. By leveraging reduced float precision, I achieved a 2x speedup without compromising accuracy. I also analyzed quantization models as a potential avenue for future improvements.
objectives: Adapt semantic segmentation models of land cover images to large datasets, ensuring computationally efficient training
Analyze parallel execution of segmentation code in multiGPU architectures
Evaluate the effectiveness of different encoder-decoder architectures on large multispectral land cover datasets
Compare segmentation results with those reported in the literature
results: MobileNetV2dilated + PPM deepsup achieved the best balance between accuracy and efficiency, with OA 85.6%, mIOU 49.0%, and mF1 61.3%
ResNet50dilated + PPM deepsup obtained similar accuracy but required longer training time
Mixed precision training reduced training time per epoch by more than half without loss of accuracy
Inference with mixed precision reached up to 2.53x speedup with no quality degradation
features: Training and inference using mixed precision (AMP-FP16)
Evaluation of five encoder-decoder models on a large-scale dataset
Integration of synchronized batch normalization for small batch sizes
Benchmarking against state-of-the-art methods on Five-Billion-Pixels dataset
challenges: Optimizing training across multiple GPUs and nodes

Original code had inefficient GPU utilization due to images of different sizes being processed simultaneously, causing synchronization delays and limiting training speedup to under 1.4× with 2 GPUs. The issue came from assuming thread execution order at the core level.
The PyTorch version used had a defective queue wrapper that triggered synchronization errors during training, requiring a custom workaround to ensure stability.
learnt-lessons: Mixed precision results in faster training and inference with no accuracy loss.
DistributedDataParallel (DDP) scales better than DataParallel (DP) in multiGPU setups
Batch size has a strong impact on both computational cost and segmentation quality
Encoder-decoder architectures can achieve state-of-the-art results on land cover segmentation

projects.title: Twitch Data Analysis (Internship at FDS)
projects.description: Worked on a complete data engineering project using real Twitch data. The idea was to figure out the best moments and conditions to place ads on the platform. It was my first chance to go through the whole data lifecycle: from raw data to insights and even predictive models.
results: Built a dimensional model in Snowflake and automated the ETL process.
Created dashboards in Power BI showing traffic, audience trends, and engagement.
Cleaned and standardized messy Twitch game names with TF-IDF and Levenshtein distance.
Developed predictive models using Facebook Prophet and a recurrent neural network.
challenges: Dealing with inconsistent and messy raw data.
Designing and building dashboards that were clear but still detailed, so that consumers could easily find insights.
Understanding how to design processes that actually scale.
learnt-lessons: Always define the right metrics before starting to build visualizations.
Data quality matters more than fancy models (garbage in, garbage out).
Feedback from more experienced teammates is gold — it saves you weeks of trial and error.
Agile methods like Kanban make it easier to stay organized and avoid stress.
Teamwork and communication are just as important as technical skills.

projects.title: Introduction to time series models
projects.description: My Mathematics Bachelor's Thesis deals with the analysis of time series from a classical statistical perspective, considering that the observed series are realisations of stochastic processes. Autoregressive (AR), moving average (MA), mixed (ARMA), integrated (ARIMA) and seasonal (SARIMA) models are presented in detail, explaining their mathematical formulation, parameter estimation methods and associated forecasting techniques. A methodology for time series analysis is also proposed, which will be used in the analysis of two real time series. In addition, a simulation study is included to illustrate and evaluate the estimation and forecasting processes of the models.

projects.title: Wine Production Forecast
projects.description: This project was part of a Datathon. The objective was  to predict wine production for a Spanish cooperative using historical vineyard and meteorological data. The goal was to estimate the harvest months in advance.
results: Identified key variables and handled missing data using advanced imputation (MICE).
Achieved improved prediction accuracy with ensemble models and feature engineering.
Best local team in the Datathon (the best in our University).
features: Exploratory data analysis and visualization of categorical and continuous variables.
Feature engineering to aggregate weather data by month and campaign.
Modeling with XGBoost, RandomForest, Linear Regression or Neural Nets.
challenges: High proportion of missing or zero values in key variables (e.g., surface area).
Temporal constraints: only pre-July meteorological data could be used for prediction.

Develop a model to predict rental prices based on various features.
results: Created a PostgreSQL relational database to store housing data
Developed multiple predictive models (linear regression, random forest, xgboost, neural networks, SVM, lasso, etc.)
Identified lasso linear regression as the best-performing model based on RMSE
Designed a dashboard and an interactive application with Streamlit and Power BI

projects.title: EA FC 25 squad builder (using ILP)
projects.description: Developed and solved and optimizacion problem to choose the best squad for EA FC 25 Ultimate Teams, given price and chemistry constraints.

projects.title: Inditex Optimization Challenge
projects.description: Optimization challenge to design a production schedule for a factory with multiple machines and item types, maximizing profit or minimizing makespan under real-world constraints. The project involves mathematical modeling, constraint programming, and the use of AMPL for industrial-scale scheduling.
objectives: Model a real-world factory production scheduling problem as a mathematical optimization problem.
Maximize profit or minimize total production time (makespan) while meeting all item demands and machine constraints.
Implement and solve the model using AMPL syntax.

projects.title: Fun Coruña webpage
projects.description: Fun Coruña is a fictional web project developed for the Web Development course in the Computer Engineering degree program. It is a platform designed to promote and manage events in the city of A Coruña, offering services tailored to both individuals and businesses.

projects.title: Messenger P2P with Java RMI
projects.description: Distributed instant messaging system developed for the Distributed Computing University course. Built with Java RMI using a peer-to-peer (P2P) architecture. The server handles client presence and notifications, while messages are exchanged directly between clients. Includes a rudimentary GUI and extended features such as authentication, dynamic friend management, and database persistence.
objectives: Design and implement a distributed system using Java RMI.
Implement a server to manage connections, presence notifications, and user registration.
Develop a peer-to-peer (P2P) messaging system where communication occurs directly between clients.
Provide a rudimentary GUI for message exchange and user interactions.

projects.title: Google Calendar Report
projects.description: Developed a solution using Python, Google API, and Power BI to automate the extraction and visualization of Google Calendar events. The project aimed to provide insights into the time spent on different activities and improve time management.
